description: Harden the pre-script feature from its POC state into a mergeable platform capability. The POC commit (990d64cb on branch poc/pre-script-feature) adds a new plugin entry point (preScript) bundled as a separate IIFE, registered via chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts at document_start in MAIN world, with values read from the adapter via the new getPreScriptValue SDK helper. The POC is assumed merged to main before these stories run. This PRD addresses the production hardening items flagged by design review: Chrome wipes registerContentScripts registrations on extension update (must re-register via chrome.runtime.onInstalled), isReady() calls from adapters cannot currently read pre-script values (plugin name is not bound outside tool-handler wraps), the side panel has no visibility into which plugins have a pre-script, open tabs do not pick up registration changes without reload, payload validation of preScriptFile is insufficient, and the E2E suite covers only the happy path. After all stories land: chrome.scripting registrations survive extension updates, getPreScriptValue works from isReady() and tool handlers, the side panel PluginCard shows a document_start badge for plugins with a pre-script, plugin.update auto-reloads matching tabs when preScriptHash changes, preScriptFile paths are validated against an adapters/ prefix and extension allowlist, the E2E suite covers update/removal/isolation/no-pre-script-fallback cases, and platform CLAUDE.md files document the new capability.

userStories.title: Re-register pre-script content scripts on extension install/update/startup
userStories.description: Call syncPreScripts(metas) from platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts after reinjectStoredPlugins() in the chrome.runtime.onInstalled and chrome.runtime.onStartup handlers, and on the module's top-level initialization path. Chrome wipes dynamic content-script registrations on extension update regardless of persistAcrossSessions:true, so without this, pre-scripts silently stop firing after any extension reload.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts chrome.runtime.onInstalled handler calls syncPreScripts with the full list returned by getAllPluginMeta
platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts chrome.runtime.onStartup handler calls syncPreScripts with the full list returned by getAllPluginMeta
platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts top-level initialization (alongside reinjectStoredPlugins) calls syncPreScripts with the full list returned by getAllPluginMeta
syncPreScripts is imported from './pre-script-registration.js' in background.ts
Each syncPreScripts invocation is wrapped in catch(err => console.warn('[opentabs] pre-script registration failed:', err)) like the existing reinjectStoredPlugins calls

userStories.title: Tighten preScriptFile payload validation
userStories.description: Change validatePluginPayload in platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts to require preScriptFile values to start with 'adapters/' and end with '.js'. Invalid values are coerced to undefined instead of rejected (consistent with how adapterFile is validated today). Add the same guard to adapterFile so we don't regress on defense-in-depth.
acceptanceCriteria: validatePluginPayload returns undefined for preScriptFile when the incoming value is not a string OR does not start with 'adapters/' OR does not end with '.js'
validatePluginPayload returns undefined for adapterFile under the same conditions
preScriptFile and adapterFile assignments in the returned object use the validated value, not the raw obj.preScriptFile / obj.adapterFile
Validation uses string prefix/suffix checks (startsWith/endsWith), not regex
No other fields in ValidatedPluginPayload are tightened
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (tighten preScriptFile + adapterFile validation in validatePluginPayload)

Context:
The POC accepts any string for preScriptFile without validating its shape. A hostile or buggy server payload could point it at an arbitrary file inside the extension bundle (e.g., 'dist/background.js'), which would then be registered as a document_start MAIN-world content script on matching tabs — a security-meaningful escalation. The existing adapterFile field has the same permissive validation (accepts any string) and should be tightened at the same time to keep the policy consistent.

Current code (lines 346-350 of message-router.ts):
```
    sourcePath: typeof obj.sourcePath === 'string' ? obj.sourcePath : undefined,
    adapterHash: typeof obj.adapterHash === 'string' ? obj.adapterHash : undefined,
    adapterFile: typeof obj.adapterFile === 'string' ? obj.adapterFile : undefined,
    preScriptFile: typeof obj.preScriptFile === 'string' ? obj.preScriptFile : undefined,
    preScriptHash: typeof obj.preScriptHash === 'string' ? obj.preScriptHash : undefined,
```

Changes:
1. Above the return object (near the start of validatePluginPayload), add:
```
    const isAdapterPath = (v: unknown): v is string =>
      typeof v === 'string' && v.startsWith('adapters/') && v.endsWith('.js');
```
2. Change the two assignments:
```
    adapterFile: isAdapterPath(obj.adapterFile) ? obj.adapterFile : undefined,
    preScriptFile: isAdapterPath(obj.preScriptFile) ? obj.preScriptFile : undefined,
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change preScriptHash validation — hashes are content-addressed strings with no path meaning, the typeof check is sufficient.
- Do NOT change sourcePath validation — it's an arbitrary filesystem path by design (local plugin dev).
- Do NOT add a length check or a character-set check beyond prefix/suffix — the server-side writePreScriptFile already produces names like 'adapters/<name>-prescript-<hash8>.js', and overly strict validation would reject valid content-hashed filenames.
- Do NOT export isAdapterPath — it stays local to validatePluginPayload.

Related code to read (do NOT modify): platform/mcp-server/src/adapter-files.ts writePreScriptFile produces filenames of the form `adapters/<pluginName>-prescript-<hash8>.js`. writeAdapterFile produces `adapters/<pluginName>-<hash8>.js`. Both satisfy the new 'adapters/' prefix + '.js' suffix requirement.

Trap warning: Do NOT convert this to a path-traversal check using the 'path' module — Node.js path APIs are not available in the extension bundle and would crash at runtime. String prefix/suffix is sufficient.

userStories.title: Bind plugin name in the adapter's isReady wrap so getPreScriptValue works outside tool calls
userStories.description: In platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts, modify the generated adapter IIFE wrapper so it wraps plugin.isReady the same way it wraps tool.handle — pinning globalThis.__openTabs._pluginName = <name> before calling the user's isReady. Currently _pluginName is only set inside the tool.handle wrapper, so an isReady() that calls getPreScriptValue returns undefined even when a value was captured.
acceptanceCriteria: bundleIIFE wrapper code wraps plugin.isReady with a function that sets globalThis.__openTabs._pluginName to the plugin name before calling the original
The wrap is inserted before the tool.handle wrap loop, so isReady is wrapped exactly once regardless of how many tools are defined
The wrap preserves the return value and propagates any thrown error from the original isReady
The wrap is no-op when plugin.isReady is not defined (defensive — the base class declares it abstract, but do not assume)
No other lifecycle hooks (onActivate, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd, teardown, onDeactivate) are wrapped for _pluginName binding — this story is scoped to isReady only

userStories.title: Surface hasPreScript in ConfigStatePlugin and the server payload
userStories.description: Add an optional hasPreScript boolean to ConfigStatePlugin in platform/shared/src/index.ts. Populate it in buildConfigStatePayload / serializePluginForExtension helpers in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts from the RegisteredPlugin.preScript field (true when plugin.preScript is a non-empty string). The side panel consumes this in a later story.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStatePlugin interface in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an optional hasPreScript?: boolean field

serializePluginForExtension in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts sets hasPreScript: true when plugin.preScript is a non-empty string; omits the field otherwise (matches the existing '...(x ? { x } : {})' pattern)
buildConfigStatePayload passes hasPreScript through to the ConfigStatePlugin shape without recomputing it
No server logic depends on hasPreScript yet — this is a pure payload-field addition
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add hasPreScript field to ConfigStatePlugin)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (populate hasPreScript in serializePluginForExtension + flow through buildConfigStatePayload)

Context:
The side panel needs to know whether a plugin declares a pre-script so it can render an indicator. PluginMeta on the extension side already has preScriptFile, but the side panel reads ConfigStatePlugin via the config.getState JSON-RPC response from the server, not PluginMeta. So we need hasPreScript on ConfigStatePlugin. Using a boolean (rather than exposing the file path) hides an implementation detail and keeps the payload small.

Current ConfigStatePlugin (platform/shared/src/index.ts lines 324-361):
```
export interface ConfigStatePlugin {
  name: string;
  displayName: string;
  ...
  /** Resolved setting values for this plugin. url fields store the full Record<string, string> map. */
  resolvedSettings?: Record<string, unknown>;
  /** Instance name → Chrome match pattern mapping for multi-instance url settings */
  instanceMap?: Record<string, string>;
}
```

Current serializePluginForExtension (platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts lines 152-230) returns a shape with adapterHash, icons, tools, etc. It does NOT today include a preScript-related field.

Changes:
1. In platform/shared/src/index.ts ConfigStatePlugin interface, add BEFORE the closing brace:
```
  /** Whether this plugin declares a pre-script (runs at document_start in MAIN world). Used by the side panel to render an indicator. */
  hasPreScript?: boolean;
```
2. In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts serializePluginForExtension:
   - Add hasPreScript to the function's return-type annotation (the inline type right after the function signature).
   - In the returned object, add `...(plugin.preScript ? { hasPreScript: true } : {}),` alongside the other conditional spreads.
3. In buildConfigStatePayload (same file), nothing needs to change IF the existing code spreads serializePluginForExtension output into each ConfigStatePlugin entry. Read the function to confirm and only modify it if the spread isn't in place.

Constraints:
- Do NOT expose the preScript content or file path in ConfigStatePlugin. Only the boolean.
- Do NOT add hasPreScript to the sync.full / plugin.update wire payloads — those already include preScriptFile (which implies presence); the boolean is a side-panel-specific convenience.
- Do NOT change the RegisteredPlugin type in state.ts — it already has preScript?: string and preScriptHash?: string from the POC commit.
- Do NOT rename or restructure existing fields in ConfigStatePlugin.

Related code to read (do NOT modify): platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts RegisteredPlugin interface lines 95-126 — confirms preScript?: string and preScriptHash?: string fields exist after the POC commit.

userStories.title: Show a pre-script indicator in the side panel PluginCard
userStories.description: Add a small indicator next to the plugin name in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx when plugin.hasPreScript is true. Use the Zap lucide icon with a tooltip explaining the behavior. Place it adjacent to the existing DEV and unreviewed badges.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.tsx imports Zap from 'lucide-react' alongside the existing ChevronDown, Settings, ShieldQuestionMark imports

When plugin.hasPreScript is true, the PluginCard renders a Tooltip-wrapped Zap icon after the plugin.displayName and before the DEV / unreviewed badges (or after them — any consistent ordering is fine as long as it's adjacent)
The Zap icon uses the same className pattern as the existing ShieldQuestionMark: 'inline-block h-3.5 w-3.5 align-middle text-muted-foreground'
The Tooltip content is 'Runs a pre-script at document_start in MAIN world'
The indicator is NOT rendered when plugin.hasPreScript is undefined or false
PluginCard.stories.tsx gets a new story variant that sets hasPreScript: true so the indicator is visible in Storybook
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (render Zap indicator when hasPreScript)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx (add a 'WithPreScript' story)

Context:
Plugins with a pre-script have extra runtime reach — they execute at document_start in MAIN world, earlier than normal adapters. Users reviewing the side panel should see that at a glance. The existing DEV badge and unreviewed ShieldQuestionMark icon are the precedent for inline indicators next to the plugin name.

Current code (PluginCard.tsx lines 242-262):
```
        <AccordionPrimitive.Trigger
          disabled={isUnconfigured}
          className={`focus-ring flex min-w-0 flex-1 items-center gap-2 py-2 pr-0 pl-2 [&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180 ${isUnconfigured ? 'cursor-default' : 'cursor-pointer'}`}>
          <div
            className={`flex min-w-0 flex-1 items-center gap-1.5 truncate font-head text-sm ${inactive ? 'text-muted-foreground' : 'text-foreground'}`}>
            {plugin.displayName}
            {plugin.source === 'local' && (
              <Badge variant="default" size="sm" className="align-middle">
                DEV
              </Badge>
            )}
            {!plugin.reviewed && (
              <Tooltip>
                <Tooltip.Trigger asChild>
                  <ShieldQuestionMark className="inline-block h-3.5 w-3.5 align-middle text-muted-foreground" />
                </Tooltip.Trigger>
                <Tooltip.Content>This plugin version has not been reviewed</Tooltip.Content>
              </Tooltip>
            )}
```

Changes:
1. Update the import on line 5 from:
   `import { ChevronDown, Settings, ShieldQuestionMark } from 'lucide-react';`
   to:
   `import { ChevronDown, Settings, ShieldQuestionMark, Zap } from 'lucide-react';`
2. After the unreviewed `<Tooltip>...ShieldQuestionMark...</Tooltip>` block (line ~260), add:
```
            {plugin.hasPreScript && (
              <Tooltip>
                <Tooltip.Trigger asChild>
                  <Zap className="inline-block h-3.5 w-3.5 align-middle text-muted-foreground" />
                </Tooltip.Trigger>
                <Tooltip.Content>Runs a pre-script at document_start in MAIN world</Tooltip.Content>
              </Tooltip>
            )}
```
3. In PluginCard.stories.tsx, add a new export alongside existing stories:
```
export const WithPreScript: Story = {
  args: {
    plugin: { ...defaultPlugin, hasPreScript: true },
  },
};
```
where `defaultPlugin` matches the args of an existing story (usually `Default.args.plugin`). Copy the exact shape from the nearest existing story — do NOT construct a plugin object from scratch.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the existing DEV or unreviewed badges.
- Do NOT reorder the existing badges — append the new one at the end of the chain inside the same outer div.
- Do NOT change the Zap icon's size class — it MUST match the ShieldQuestionMark's 'h-3.5 w-3.5' so the three indicators align.
- Do NOT create a new reusable component — this is a 7-line conditional block following the established pattern.

Related code to read (do NOT modify): platform/shared/src/index.ts ConfigStatePlugin interface — confirm hasPreScript field was added in US-004. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts — PluginState extends ConfigStatePlugin, so the new field flows through automatically.

Trap warning: Do NOT add hasPreScript to a separate fetch or bridge message — it rides on the existing config.getState / plugins.changed payload already consumed by the side panel.

userStories.title: Auto-reload matching tabs when plugin.update changes preScriptHash
userStories.description: In platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts handlePluginUpdate, after upsertPreScript completes, compare the incoming meta.preScriptHash against the previously-stored hash. If they differ AND a pre-script is declared on the new meta, call chrome.tabs.reload on every matching tab. This ensures already-open tabs pick up a changed pre-script instead of silently running the old one until the user reloads manually.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginUpdate reads the existing PluginMeta (if any) BEFORE calling storePluginsBatch([meta])

After storePluginsBatch + upsertPreScript, if existingMeta?.preScriptHash !== meta.preScriptHash AND meta.preScriptFile is defined, chrome.tabs.query is called with meta.urlPatterns and every returned tab.id is passed to chrome.tabs.reload
Initial install (existingMeta === undefined) with a declared pre-script also triggers the reload — the 'differ' check is !existingMeta || existingMeta.preScriptHash !== meta.preScriptHash
Reload errors are caught per-tab and logged with console.warn; a single tab's failure must NOT block reloads of other tabs
handleSyncFull does NOT auto-reload — sync.full happens on every WebSocket reconnect (potentially often) and must not reload every matching tab each time
Tab reload happens only in handlePluginUpdate

userStories.title: Widen the pre-script E2E suite to cover update, removal, isolation, and no-pre-script fallback
userStories.description: Rename e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts to e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts and expand it from one test to four. The existing happy-path test stays as test 1. Add tests for: plugin update with changed preScriptHash triggers tab reload; plugin uninstall unregisters the content script; two plugins with pre-scripts have isolated namespaces; a plugin with no pre-script still works (fallback path).
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts is renamed to e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts (git mv, preserving history)
The existing happy-path test in the renamed file still passes unchanged
A new test verifies that when handlePluginUpdate fires with a changed preScriptHash, currently-open matching tabs are reloaded (observable via chrome.tabs.onUpdated or page.waitForNavigation)

A new test verifies that after chrome.scripting.unregisterContentScripts runs (simulating plugin removal), a subsequent navigation to a matching URL does NOT invoke the pre-script (preScript namespace stays empty)

A new test verifies two plugins with pre-scripts writing to the same key ('authToken') end up with isolated buckets — plugin-a reads its own value, plugin-b reads its own value, no cross-contamination
A new test verifies getPreScriptValue returns undefined when a plugin has no preScript declared (regression guard — e2e-test plugin path)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts → e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts (rename via git mv)
- e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts (expand from 1 test to 4)
- plugins/prescript-test/ (existing POC plugin — stays as fixture)
- e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts (existing POC mock server — stays as fixture)
- Possibly add a second small plugin fixture plugins/prescript-test-b/ for the isolation test

Context:
The POC landed one happy-path test: a pre-script captures a bearer token from the mock server's inline <script>, the adapter reads it back, and the tool returns it. That's the critical path. This story adds coverage for the failure modes flagged during design review:
1. Pre-script hash changes → open tabs must reload (US-006's behavior).
2. Plugin uninstall → content script unregistered (no leak).
3. Multiple plugins → namespace isolation (no cross-contamination).
4. No pre-script → adapter falls through gracefully (no regression for existing plugins).

Current e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts structure:
- One test.describe block with one test.
- Uses fixtures.ts helpers (startMcpServer, launchExtensionContext, createMcpClient, etc.).
- Spawns e2e/prescript-mock-server.ts via startMockServer helper defined inline.
- Opens a tab, asserts on preScript namespace, asserts on tool result.

Changes:
1. `git mv e2e/prescript-poc.e2e.ts e2e/pre-script.e2e.ts` (preserves git history).
2. Rename the describe block from 'Pre-script POC' to 'Pre-script feature'.
3. Keep the existing test as 'pre-script captures bearer token before page overwrites window.fetch'.
4. Add test 2 ('pre-script update reloads matching tabs'):
   - Start the same fixture setup.
   - Open a matching tab; assert captured token = mock.expectedToken.
   - Send a plugin.update message to the extension directly via the server's WebSocket with a modified preScriptHash (simplest: call the MCP server's /reload HTTP endpoint after modifying the plugin's source to change its content hash, OR construct a plugin.update JSON-RPC message and relay via context.serviceWorker evaluate of chrome.runtime.sendMessage). Use whichever is simpler given fixtures; prefer a direct SW-side dispatch if available.
   - Set up a page.on('load') listener BEFORE the update to detect navigation.
   - Verify the tab reloaded (listener fired) within 5 seconds.
5. Add test 3 ('plugin uninstall unregisters pre-script'):
   - Start fixture, register plugin, verify chrome.scripting.getRegisteredContentScripts() contains 'opentabs-pre-prescript-test'.
   - Send plugin.uninstall for 'prescript-test' via the MCP client (or a bg: relay message).
   - Assert getRegisteredContentScripts no longer contains 'opentabs-pre-prescript-test'.
6. Add test 4 ('multiple plugins have isolated pre-script namespaces'):
   - Create a second minimal plugin fixture at plugins/prescript-test-b/ (or dynamically via createMinimalPlugin helper in fixtures.ts if it supports preScript — check first).
   - Both plugins write 'authToken' to their namespace (different values).
   - Open a tab matching both plugins' urlPatterns.
   - Assert globalThis.__openTabs.preScript['prescript-test'].authToken === value-a.
   - Assert globalThis.__openTabs.preScript['prescript-test-b'].authToken === value-b.
7. Add test 5 ('plugin without pre-script — getPreScriptValue returns undefined'):
   - Reuse the e2e-test plugin (which has no preScript).
   - Open the standard test-server tab and wait for the adapter.
   - page.evaluate(() => { const ot = globalThis.__openTabs; return ot?.preScript?.['e2e-test']; }) — expect it to be undefined.
   - Optional: call a tool in that plugin that internally calls getPreScriptValue('anything') and verify it returns undefined in the tool output.

Constraints:
- Do NOT delete plugins/prescript-test/ — it's the primary fixture and tests 1, 2, 3 depend on it.
- Do NOT inline plugins/prescript-test-b/ if adding it requires more than creating the same 5-file structure as prescript-test/. If it's too heavy, skip test 4 and document why in a comment — but prefer shipping it.
- Do NOT make the tests serial via test.describe.serial unless they truly cannot run in parallel. Each test should spin its own fixtures.
- Do NOT reuse createMinimalPlugin without reading it first — it may not support preScript yet.
- Do NOT add unit tests for the pre-script-registration.ts module here — that would be out of scope; this is an E2E-focused story.

Trap warning: startMockServer in the existing POC test uses an inline spawn helper. Do NOT try to use fixtures.ts startServerProcess directly — that one is specialized to the e2e-test server's control API (reset/set-auth). Keep the local helper but extract it to a top-level const in the file so all four tests reuse it.

userStories.title: Document the pre-script feature in platform CLAUDE.md files
userStories.description: Add new sections to platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md (authoring a pre-script), platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md (registration lifecycle and auto-reload policy), and platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md (new wire fields). Add a one-line pointer to the root CLAUDE.md Key Concepts section.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md gets a new 'Pre-Scripts' section documenting: what a pre-script is, when to use one, the @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script subpath export, the definePreScript({ set, log }) API, and the adapter-side getPreScriptValue<T>(key) reader

platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md gets a new paragraph documenting: the syncPreScripts call chain (onInstalled, onStartup, module init, handleSyncFull, handlePluginUpdate), the note that Chrome wipes registrations on extension update, and the plugin.update auto-reload policy

platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md gets a new paragraph documenting: the preScriptFile and preScriptHash fields added to sync.full and plugin.update payloads, and that the server writes {plugin}-prescript-{hash8}.js files to the adapters/ directory

Root CLAUDE.md Key Concepts section gets one new line: '**Pre-scripts**: optional plugin entry point running at document_start in MAIN world via chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts; see platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md.'
Each section follows the existing CLAUDE.md style — no emojis, no hype, factual and terse
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md (add 'Pre-Scripts' section)
- platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md (add paragraph on registration lifecycle)
- platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md (add paragraph on wire fields)
- CLAUDE.md (add one line to Key Concepts)

Context:
These docs are read by AI coding agents (Claude Code) and human contributors. They must be accurate and terse — no preamble, no marketing copy. Keep each addition to ~8-15 lines. The existing headings and style are the template.

Changes:

1. platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md — add the following section near the end (after 'Why Resources and Prompts Are Not Supported'):
```
## Pre-Scripts

Plugins can declare an optional `preScript` path in their `package.json` opentabs field. The pre-script runs at `document_start` in MAIN world via `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts` — before any page JavaScript, including inline `<script>` tags in `<head>`. Use a pre-script only when the adapter (which runs at `document_idle`) is too late — e.g., capturing transient state like auth tokens from the page's initial fetches, before the page wraps or hides them.

**Authoring:** The pre-script imports from a narrow subpath, `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk/pre-script`, which exports only `definePreScript(fn)`. The callback receives `{ set, log }` — `set(key, value)` writes to this plugin's namespace on `globalThis.__openTabs.preScript[<pluginName>]`, and `log` logs to the browser console with a plugin-scoped prefix. Pre-scripts have NO access to DOM helpers, fetch utilities, or tool infrastructure — those assume a ready DOM.

**Reading from the adapter:** The main SDK exports `getPreScriptValue<T>(key): T | undefined` which reads from the current plugin's namespace. It returns `undefined` when the pre-script has not declared one, the adapter was injected into a tab that was already open when the plugin registered (pre-scripts only fire on future navigations), or the pre-script ran but did not call `set(key, ...)`. Callers MUST handle the `undefined` case.
```

2. platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md — add after an existing section (e.g., after 'Tab State Machine' or 'Tool Dispatch'):
```
### Pre-Script Registration

When a plugin declares a `preScript`, the server writes its IIFE to `adapters/<name>-prescript-<hash8>.js` and forwards `preScriptFile` + `preScriptHash` in sync.full and plugin.update payloads. The extension calls `syncPreScripts` (from `pre-script-registration.ts`) on every path that can load or refresh plugin metadata: `handleSyncFull`, `handlePluginUpdate`, `handlePluginUninstall`, and the three lifecycle entry points in `background.ts` (`chrome.runtime.onInstalled`, `chrome.runtime.onStartup`, and module-top-level initialization).

The redundant lifecycle calls are NOT defensive belts-and-braces — Chrome wipes `registerContentScripts` registrations on extension update regardless of `persistAcrossSessions: true`, so the `onInstalled` handler is the only reliable way to restore them. The module-top-level call covers the case where plugins are stored in `chrome.storage.local` but no WebSocket event has fired yet.

On `plugin.update` where the incoming `preScriptHash` differs from the stored hash, `message-router.ts` additionally reloads all matching tabs via `chrome.tabs.reload`. This is skipped on `sync.full` (which fires on every WebSocket reconnect) and on adapter-only changes (where forced re-injection suffices).
```

3. platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md — add a short paragraph to an appropriate existing section (e.g., 'Plugin Discovery' or near the wire-format descriptions):
```
### Pre-Script Files

Plugins that declare `preScript` in `package.json` ship a second IIFE at `dist/pre-script.iife.js` alongside the adapter. On sync, the server writes it to `~/.opentabs/extension/adapters/<plugin>-prescript-<hash8>.js` via `writePreScriptFile` (parallel to `writeAdapterFile`) and includes `preScriptFile` (relative path) plus `preScriptHash` (sha256) in the `sync.full` and `plugin.update` wire payloads. The extension registers these via `chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts`; the server does not interact with the Chrome scripting API directly.
```

4. CLAUDE.md (root) — find the 'Key Concepts' heading and add ONE new bullet/line after the existing bullets:
```
**Pre-scripts**: Optional plugin entry point running at document_start in MAIN world via chrome.scripting.registerContentScripts. See platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md.
```
Copy the exact style of the existing bullets (some end with periods, some are paragraphs — match whatever's there).

Constraints:
- Do NOT add emojis, marketing language, or 'getting started' tutorial content. These are internal engineering notes.
- Do NOT duplicate content across the three CLAUDE.md files — each file covers the concepts relevant to its package.
- Do NOT rewrite any existing sections.
- Do NOT add examples longer than 5 lines — the referenced code is in the repo and can be read there.
- Do NOT add forward references to TODOs or upcoming features.
